highlights.text: However, as I discuss freqently those deep “elevator down” selloffs have a sibling, and these two siblings don’t spend much time away from each other: The Short Squeeze.
Short squeezes are caused by my core setup, the Failed Breakdown, and first thing on Monday morning, we started on this and it ran into today.

A Failed Breakdown here would be very much of interest and this remains a strong zone .” On Monday morning we lost 5565 (March 13th multi-week lows), sold to 5534, recovered 5565, and longs were triggered for the week .
For those who missed that entry though, the market is generous and gave us another Failed Breakdown yesterday and *again* this morning for good measure.
Since bears control I am in no rush to insta-buy this or any support (my preference is always high quality Failed Breakdowns).

Its why I wrote yesterday: “We are in a rally leg that began on Monday with the failed breakdown of 5565…We could fill this out in many different pathways but as long as 5620 holds, we continue up to 5695, 5720-25.
Test there, and if that clears, the next squeeze leg begins to 5770, 5797.” This morning we tested 5620, put in another classic Failed Breakdown to get us long, then tagged 5720-25+ by noon.
In today’s newsletter I’ll talk this, I’ll do a deep dive into the Failed Breakdown we saw Monday that started the recent leg up, and yesterday to continue it.

post_title: Monster Squeeze For SPX. Bottom In, Or Trap? Tariffs Decide. April 3rd Plan
